title: Moroccan Sea Bass Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic Moroccan-inspired dish featuring sea bass cooked in a rich tamarind and sour sauce.
instructions: Season the sea bass fillets with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe tagine or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the sea bass fillets for 2-3 minutes on each side, then remove from heat.
Add the tamarind paste, sour cherries, and moroccan spice mix to the tagine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Return the sea bass fillets to the tagine and cover with a lid.
Transfer the tagine to the preheated oven and bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
Serve the sea bass with the tamarind sauce spooned over the top.
